tweets.title: The U.S. Marine Corps has forward deployed NMESIS anti-ship missile systems and MADIS air defence systems to Okinawa, Japan, while also rehearsing deployments to the Luzon Strait in the Philippines. The deployments are part of the Pentagon's Indo-Pacific strategy to strengthen deterrence and improve the ability to defend Taiwan in the event of a potential conflict. Positioned along the First Island Chain, these systems are designed to: - Deny hostile naval access with NMESIS anti-ship missiles - Defend against drones and aircraft using MADIS - Enhance interoperability with regional allies through joint exercises
tweets.content:
The U.S. Marine Corps has forward deployed NMESIS anti-ship missile systems and MADIS air defence systems to Okinawa, Japan, while also rehearsing deployments to the Luzon Strait in the Philippines.

The deployments are part of the Pentagon's Indo-Pacific strategy to strengthen deterrence and improve the ability to defend Taiwan in the event of a potential conflict.

Positioned along the First Island Chain, these systems are designed to:

- Deny hostile naval access with NMESIS anti-ship missiles

- Defend against drones and aircraft using MADIS

- Enhance interoperability with regional allies through joint exercises

tweets.content:
UP Defence Corridor Attracts ₹39,571 Crore in Investments

The Uttar Pradesh Defence Industrial Corridor (UPDIC) has attracted ₹39,571 crore in investment proposals, with 65 companies onboard and 9 defence manufacturing units already operational.

Highlights:

- ₹13,486 crore worth of projects already grounded

- Six defence hubs: Kanpur, Jhansi, Lucknow, Aligarh, Agra & Chitrakoot

- Major companies including Adani Defence & Aerospace and BrahMos Aerospace have begun production

tweets.title: ARMY CHIEF UNVEILS 'VIJAY' VISION FOR A FUTURE-READY INDIAN ARMY Chief of the Army Staff Gen Dhiraj Seth outlined his strategic roadmap for the Indian Army under the acronym "VIJAY", aimed at building a technology-enabled, future-ready and self-reliant force. VIJAY stands for: V – Vigilance & Readiness I – Innovation & Transformation J – Jointness & IntegratioA – Aatmanirbharta (Self-Reliance) Y – Yoddha First (Soldier First) Gen Seth said the Army will focus on operational readiness, technological innovation, tri-service integration, indigenous capability development, and the welfare of every soldier, from the newest Agniveer to the most senior veteran, while supporting the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.
tweets.content:
ARMY CHIEF UNVEILS 'VIJAY' VISION FOR A FUTURE-READY INDIAN ARMY

Chief of the Army Staff Gen Dhiraj Seth outlined his strategic roadmap for the Indian Army under the acronym "VIJAY", aimed at building a technology-enabled, future-ready and self-reliant force.

VIJAY stands for:
V – Vigilance & Readiness
I – Innovation & Transformation
J – Jointness & IntegratioA – Aatmanirbharta (Self-Reliance)
Y – Yoddha First (Soldier First)

Gen Seth said the Army will focus on operational readiness, technological innovation, tri-service integration, indigenous capability development, and the welfare of every soldier, from the newest Agniveer to the most senior veteran, while supporting the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.

tweets.title: Defence Research & Development Laboratory (DRDL), Hyderabad, is planning to establish a state-of-the-art blow-down type Trisonic Wind Tunnel (TWT) with a 1.6 m × 1.6 m test section, capable of operating across a Mach number range of 0.2 to 4.5. The facility will be developed at a designated site in Hyderabad, India, to provide controlled, uniform, and steady airflow conditions for aerodynamic testing. It will support the evaluation of scaled models of rockets, tactical missiles, aircraft, launch vehicles, and other aerospace systems, enabling the generation of critical aerodynamic data across subsonic, transonic, and supersonic flight regimes.
tweets.content:
Defence Research & Development Laboratory (DRDL), Hyderabad, is planning to establish a state-of-the-art blow-down type Trisonic Wind Tunnel (TWT) with a 1.6 m × 1.6 m test section, capable of operating across a Mach number range of 0.2 to 4.5.

The facility will be developed at a designated site in Hyderabad, India, to provide controlled, uniform, and steady airflow conditions for aerodynamic testing. It will support the evaluation of scaled models of rockets, tactical missiles, aircraft, launch vehicles, and other aerospace systems, enabling the generation of critical aerodynamic data across subsonic, transonic, and supersonic flight regimes.

tweets.content:
Operation Amistad: Indian Army Field Hospital in Venezuela

A local resident details her experience receiving medical care at the Indian Army's field hospital in Caracas, established following the recent 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes.

Indian Army Relief Efforts:

1️⃣Rapid Deployment: A medical contingent from the 60 Para Field Hospital was airlifted to Venezuela to provide immediate emergency and trauma care.

2️⃣ Advanced Facilities: The deployment utilizes BHISHM Cubes, rapidly deployable mobile hospitals designed for critical disaster response.

3️⃣Round-the-Clock Operations: Experienced Indian Army medical officers are providing comprehensive care 24/7.

4️⃣ Free Medical Assistance: All consultations, treatments, and prescribed medications are provided entirely free of charge to those impacted by the disaster.

The ongoing deployment highlights India's commitment to global humanitarian assistance and disaster relief.

tweets.title: BREAKING: Pakistan Opposition Leaders Blocked from Entering Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK) The Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JKJAAC) has accused Pakistani authorities of suppressing democratic rights after a delegation of opposition leaders was reportedly stopped from entering Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK). According to JKJAAC, the delegation, led by Mahmood Khan Achakzai and including former Prime Minister Shahid Khaqan Abbasi and other opposition figures, was halted at Kotli and prevented from proceeding further. The committee also alleged restrictions on movement and supplies, describing them as part of a broader crackdown on dissent. JKJAAC reiterated that its movement is peaceful and vowed to continue protests until its demands are addressed.

BREAKING: Pakistan Opposition Leaders Blocked from Entering Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK)

The Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JKJAAC) has accused Pakistani authorities of suppressing democratic rights after a delegation of opposition leaders was reportedly stopped from entering Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK).

According to JKJAAC, the delegation, led by Mahmood Khan Achakzai and including former Prime Minister Shahid Khaqan Abbasi and other opposition figures, was halted at Kotli and prevented from proceeding further.

The committee also alleged restrictions on movement and supplies, describing them as part of a broader crackdown on dissent. JKJAAC reiterated that its movement is peaceful and vowed to continue protests until its demands are addressed.

tweets.title: General Upendra Dwivedi concludes his tenure as the 30th Chief of Army Staff, handing over command to General Dhiraj Seth. His tenure marked a distinct operational shift for the Indian Army, transitioning it toward a highly modernized, outcome-driven force. Key highlights from his leadership: - The Five Pillars: Gen Dwivedi executed a transformation framework focused on technology absorption, agile structural reorganization, and advanced human resource upskilling. - Tech & Self-Reliance: Championed indigenous defense manufacturing, overseeing the integration of roughly 50,000 drones alongside AI, big data, and quantum tech into daily operations. - Combat Readiness: Validated new doctrines and maintained strict vigilance through major deployments, notably Operation Snow Leopard on the northern borders and Operation Sindoor on the western front. - Tri-Service Synergy: Accelerated joint interoperability among the Army, Navy, and Air Force, laying critical groundwork for theaterization. - Specialized Units: Raised specialized combat formations explicitly trained to handle multi-domain threats and mass drone warfare.
tweets.content:
General Upendra Dwivedi concludes his tenure as the 30th Chief of Army Staff, handing over command to General Dhiraj Seth. His tenure marked a distinct operational shift for the Indian Army, transitioning it toward a highly modernized, outcome-driven force.

Key highlights from his leadership:

- The Five Pillars: Gen Dwivedi executed a transformation framework focused on technology absorption, agile structural reorganization, and advanced human resource upskilling.

- Tech & Self-Reliance: Championed indigenous defense manufacturing, overseeing the integration of roughly 50,000 drones alongside AI, big data, and quantum tech into daily operations.

- Combat Readiness: Validated new doctrines and maintained strict vigilance through major deployments, notably Operation Snow Leopard on the northern borders and Operation Sindoor on the western front.

- Tri-Service Synergy: Accelerated joint interoperability among the Army, Navy, and Air Force, laying critical groundwork for theaterization.

- Specialized Units: Raised specialized combat formations explicitly trained to handle multi-domain threats and mass drone warfare.

BREAKING: Arunachal Tribal Body Alleges Chinese Encroachment

The Nah Welfare Society (NWS) in Arunachal Pradesh has alleged that the PLA has gradually occupied parts of its traditional grazing, hunting, and agricultural land in Upper Subansiri district over the past six years.

According to a memorandum submitted to district authorities, the group claims Chinese forces have established roads and military camps in several areas under the Taksing revenue circle.

No official confirmation has been issued by the district administration, the state government, or the Government of India. Local MLA Nakap Nalo has said the allegations require official verification, calling it a matter of national security.
